intro:
There is no information available on the plant Zanthoxylum nebuletorum. However, I can provide information on Zanthoxylum, a genus of plants commonly known as prickly ash or Sichuan pepper. 

The Zanthoxylum genus is a type of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, which includes citrus and rue. It is characterized by its aromatic, pungent leaves and berries.

Some species of Zanthoxylum, such as Zanthoxylum bungeanum, have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. They are believed to aid in digestion, relieve pain and reduce stress.

The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum species include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids. The most common alkaloids found in these plants include Zanthoxylum alkaloids and norisoboldine.

The most common herbal preparations made with Zanthoxylum species include tea, capsules, and essential oils. These preparations are often used to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation and alleviate stress.
